scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT COURT / CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. The Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of the Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. The Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

COMMERCIAL COURTS ACT COMPLIANCE

4. The Plaintiff states that the suit is governed by the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 as the specified value exceeds Rs. 3,00,000. The Plaintiff has complied with the requirements of Section 12A regarding pre-institution mediation as applicable.

LIMITATION

5. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. The Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{PLAINTIFF_FULL_ADDRESS}}. The Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{DEFENDANT_FULL_ADDRESS}}. The parties are known to each other and the Defendant approached the Plaintiff for financial assistance.

7. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) to the Defendant as a hand loan. The said amount was transferred via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456. A copy of the bank transfer record is annexed herewith as Annexure A.

8. Although there was no written agreement or promissory note executed between the parties, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and the liability to repay the same via WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties. Copies of the relevant WhatsApp messages are annexed herewith as Annexure B.

9. The transaction was purely based on trust and the verbal understanding between the parties. The Plaintiff retained the proof of transfer and digital acknowledgment as evidence of the debt owed by the Defendant.

10. The Plaintiff repeatedly demanded the repayment of the loan amount from the Defendant on {{DATES_OF_ORAL_DEMANDS}}. The Defendant assured the Plaintiff of repayment but failed to honor the commitment.

11. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} to the Defendant calling upon him to repay the outstanding dues. A copy of the legal notice is annexed herewith as Annexure C.

12. Despite the receipt of the legal notice and the acknowledged liability, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the loan amount. The Defendant has committed breach of the obligation to repay the money lent by the Plaintiff.

DAMAGES

13. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the principal amount of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- from the Defendant. The Defendant is liable to repay the said sum immediately.

14. The Plaintiff further claims interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the principal amount. The interest is claimed from the date of default, i.e., {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, till the date of realisation.

15. The total claim comprises the principal sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- along with accrued interest at {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The Plaintiff has incurred costs due to the Defendant's failure to repay, for which compensation is sought.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Defendant borrowed / received a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same with interest under Section 69 read with Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

17. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

19. The right to sue further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

20. The right to sue is not a continuing cause of
